questions.text: WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA Answer ALL TWENTY FOUR questions. Write your answers in the spaces provided. You must write down all the stages in your working. 1 The length of a ship is 142.8 m, correct to 1 decimal place. (i) Write down the lower bound of the length of the ship. .............................................................. m (1) (ii) Write down the upper bound of the length of the ship. .............................................................. m (1) (Total for Question 1 is 2 marks)
DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT W 2 Show that 2 1 4 15 7 36 7 (Total for Question 2 is 3 marks)

*P81635A0328* Turn over 3   DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA 3 Here is a biased 5-sided spinner. When the spinner is spun, it can land on blue or on green or on purple or on red or on yellow. green purple red yellow blue The table gives information about the probability of the spinner landing on each colour. Colour Probability 0.12 blue 0.20 green 0.38 purple 4x red x yellow Sophie spins the spinner once. (a) Work out the probability that the spinner lands on blue or on green or on purple. .............................................................. (1) Max spins the spinner 350 times. (b) Work out an estimate for the number of times the spinner lands on red. .............................................................. (4) (Total for Question 3 is 5 marks)

*P81635A01028* 10   DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA 10 The diagram shows a rectangular sheet of metal. Work out the pressure on the table due to the sheet of metal. Show your working clearly. .............................................................. newtons/cm2 (Total for Question 10 is 5 marks) pressure = force area

*P81635A01728* Turn over 17   DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN T 17 Kannika has 9 counters. There is a number on each counter. 1 1 2 3 3 3 5 6 6 Kannika puts the 9 counters in a bag. She takes at random a counter from the bag and does not replace the counter. She then takes at random a second counter from the bag. Work out the probability that the sum of the numbers on the two counters is less than 5 ............................................................ (Total for Question 17 is 3 marks)
